page: Vultr vhf-2c-2gb is a High Frequency Compute server configured with 2 shared vCPUs and 2.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an x86_64 Intel Core Processor based on the Skylake architecture running at 2.0 GHz, this instance features 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core. It includes a bundled 80 GB NVMe SSD for local storage and provides one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1425.29 and single-threaded gzip compression at 24.24 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory performance are more limited. The stress-ng multi-core score is 2786.09, and the Passmark CPU score is 3137.23, both falling into weaker performance tiers. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read and write speeds of 18.67 GB/sec and 12.65 GB/sec respectively, resulting in a Passmark memory score of 1171.63.

Due to its shared CPU allocation and low memory density of 1.0 GB per core, this server is positioned as a budget-friendly option for workloads that do not require sustained high-performance or large memory footprints. Database operations and static web serving throughput are relatively low, with Redis SET operations reaching 2.3 million operations per second and web serving throughput at 2.3 GB/sec. Consequently, this instance is best suited for lightweight applications, basic web serving, development environments, and simple background tasks rather than intensive database hosting or data-heavy processing.
description: The Vultr vhf-2c-2gb is a budget-friendly High Frequency Compute server featuring 2 shared vCPUs and 2.0 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 Intel Skylake architecture running at 2.0 GHz, it includes a bundled 80 GB NVMe SSD and one public IPv4 address. Benchmarks show average single-core performance, highlighted by a stress-ng single-core score of 1425.29 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 24.24 GB/sec. However, its shared CPU allocation and limited memory bandwidth—with large-block reads at 18.67 GB/sec—constrain multi-core and database performance. This resource profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for lightweight workloads, such as basic web serving, development testing, and simple background scripts, while remaining unsuitable for memory-intensive or high-throughput database applications.
og_description: Vultr vhf-2c-2gb is a High Frequency Compute server with 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 2.0 GB RAM, and an 80 GB NVMe SSD, offering a budget-friendly option for lightweight web serving and development tasks.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-2c-2gb features 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 2.0 GB RAM, and 80 GB NVMe storage, ideal for budget-friendly, lightweight utility workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared-CPU instance featuring local NVMe storage and balanced single-core performance for lightweight utility and development workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Skylake x86_64 architecture with 2 shared vCPUs and 2.0 GB RAM
Bundled 80 GB NVMe SSD local storage and one public IPv4 address
Average single-core performance suitable for basic single-threaded tasks
Constrained memory bandwidth and multi-core performance under heavy loads
Cost-effective option for light web serving, testing, and background scripts
